description: Telemetry Contract - Single Source of Truth for Block Order and Telemetry Schema

requirements: MUST appear in canonical block order
MUST expose execution status: executed | skipped | silent

status_meaning.executed: Block executed successfully (may have zero events)
status_meaning.skipped: Block was skipped due to early exit or bypass condition
status_meaning.silent: Block executed but emitted no events (normal for some blocks)
status_meaning.not_executed: Block was not executed in this turn (initial state for canonical blocks)

always_on_block.note: Always-on blocks are guaranteed to be present in every turn response, but may have zero events. This is semantically valid and does not indicate an error.

monotonic_ordering.description: Blocks must appear in canonical order

always_on_blocks.description: Always-on blocks must be present in every turn
always_on_blocks.semantics: Always-on blocks MUST appear in canonical block order, MAY emit zero events, and MUST expose execution status (executed | skipped | silent)

always_on_block_status.description: Always-on blocks must have valid status: executed, skipped, or silent
always_on_block_status.note: Status 'ok' is also valid but 'executed' is preferred for always-on blocks to distinguish from regular blocks

zero_events_valid.description: Always-on blocks may have zero events - this is semantically valid
zero_events_valid.note: A block with status 'executed' or 'silent' and zero events is valid for always-on blocks
